description_long: This is a medium-sized, pitched-roof tent constructed with a blue fabric canopy supported by a visible wooden pole frame. The tent features prominent rope bindings at each corner and along the roof ridge, giving it a rustic, hand-tied appearance. Decorative white cross-stitching runs along the edges of the fabric panels, adding a crafted detail to the overall design. The front of the tent has a triangular entrance with a small hanging wooden sign, while the rear is fully enclosed. The structure appears sturdy, with the wooden poles providing a solid framework and the fabric panels tightly stretched. The tent is likely intended for temporary shelter or as a market stall in an urban park or street fair setting.
description: A blue, pitched-roof tent with wooden poles, rope bindings, and white cross-stitching, featuring a triangular entrance and a hanging sign.
description_view_0: Side view showing the blue fabric panel with white cross-stitching and the wooden pole frame with rope bindings.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the triangular entrance, hanging wooden sign, and the converging roof poles tied with rope.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the fabric panel and pole structure without an entrance.
description_view_3: Rear view highlighting the fully enclosed back panel and the peak of the roof with rope-tied poles.
attribute_entrance_type: Triangular flap entrance at the front, secured with rope ties, with a small hanging wooden sign above.
attribute_guy_line_configuration: No external guy lines visible; structural support appears to rely on rope bindings at corners and roof ridge.
